summary:
This matches the design's file-level plan exactly, plus the required test scaffolding. Implementation is complete.

## Summary

Implemented dark mode support per the technical design in `.pipeliner/phases/02-plan/main/design-writer/output/design.md`, satisfying all 13 requirements (R1–R13):

- **Storage & default**: theme choice lives in a non-httpOnly `theme` cookie (no DB/model changes); absent cookie = follow system preference via `matchMedia`.
- **Zero-flash**: `app/views/shared/_theme_init_script.html.erb`, an inline script rendered first in `<head>` on both layouts, applies the correct class before first paint.
- **Toggle**: `app/javascript/controllers/theme_controller.js` (new Stimulus controller) flips the `dark` class, writes the cookie on manual choice, and live-reacts to system-preference changes only while no manual choice exists — rendered via `app/views/shared/_theme_toggle.html.erb` in the sidebar (all app pages) and the auth layout header (logged-out pages).
- **Styling**: `app/assets/tailwind/application.css` adds a class-based `@custom-variant dark` plus semantic color tokens (`bg-app`, `bg-surface`, `text-default`, `border-default`, etc.) via `@utility`, redeclared under `.dark`. Every view in the app was swept from raw `gray-*`/`white` classes to these tokens; `StatusHelper::TONE_CLASSES` got dark variants so status badges stay distinguishable in both themes.
- **Guide updated**: `guides/ui-style-guide.md` now documents the token table and the mandatory-tokens-over-raw-grays rule, per CLAUDE.md's guide-update requirement.
- **Tests**: `test/helpers/theme_helper_test.rb` (cookie logic) and `test/system/theme_toggle_test.rb` (first system test in the app — added `test/application_system_test_case.rb`), covering toggle-and-persist behavior.

Verification: `ruby -c` passed on all new/changed `.rb` files, `rubocop` (project config) reported zero offenses, ERB tags balance-checked clean across every touched view, and the Stimulus controller passed a No
